title: Rate Limiting
description: Per-route and global rate limiting with extended lockout support

blocks.text: Rate limiting is part of the API pipeline — no separate middleware. Set a default on your config and override per route in defineApi. Counters live in a cache adapter, so they can be shared across instances.
defaultRateLimit applies to every endpoint. A route's rateLimit overrides it for that route; false opts out entirely.

rows: Maximum requests allowed within the window.
Optional lockout duration after the limit is hit. Defaults to the remaining window.

blocks.text: For sensitive endpoints (login, OTP, password reset), blockDurationMs locks the identifier out for longer than the counting window — so five bad attempts in a minute can mean a one-hour block.

Counters are keyed ratelimit:{identifier}:{method}:{pathname}. The identifier comes from getRateLimitIdentifier(ctx) — typically a user id. Unset or undefined, it falls back to the client IP (x-forwarded-for / x-real-ip / remoteAddr).

Counters live in rateLimitCache, separate from the response cache. With multiple instances it must be shared (Redis/Deno KV), or each instance counts on its own and the real limit becomes max × instances.

Atomic increments matter here: shared adapters (Redis, Deno KV) implement an atomic incr so concurrent requests can't punch through the limit. A per-process memoryCache is safe only for single-instance deployments.
